data.body:
EXPERIMENT 1 — gitleaks/semgrep fit behind grounding-oracle Engine B (b744 WS5 / bba6). Tools: gitleaks 8.30.1, semgrep 1.168.0 (both installed/present), ast-grep 0.44.0. Engine B backends are EXACTLY 3 (registry.py:39-42): opengrep, ast-grep, metric; a detector = native matcher YAML rule + metadata.rebar_envelope, parsed via yaml.safe_load (registry.py:236-252); opengrep backend runs 'opengrep scan --sarif' and re-injects the envelope into rule.properties before SARIF parse (engine_b.py:297-329).
PROVEN: (a) semgrep passes arbitrary metadata (incl. a planted rebar_envelope) THROUGH VERBATIM to its JSON output extra.metadata.rebar_envelope, and emits SARIF 2.1.0 (tool 'Semgrep OSS'). opengrep is a semgrep fork sharing the YAML-rule format -> semgrep detectors drop into Engine B natively as detector YAML; this is the native path, no adapter needed.
(b) gitleaks uses TOML config (--config .toml / GITLEAKS_CONFIG_TOML) with rules COMPILED INTO the binary (no per-rule YAML file you can point --config at), so it CANNOT be expressed as an Engine B detector YAML (yaml.safe_load would reject TOML). BUT gitleaks emits SARIF 2.1.0 (schema json.schemastore.org/sarif-2.1.0) and JSON (keys RuleID/File/StartLine/EndLine/Match/Secret/Entropy/Fingerprint). So wrapping gitleaks needs EITHER a 4th backend (BACKEND_GITLEAKS subprocess adapter) OR a generic SARIF-ingest backend that normalizes gitleaks (and any SARIF tool) -> evidence via sarif.from_sarif(trust_rebar_bag=False). The latter is the cleaner choice since semgrep ALSO emits SARIF.
(c) CRITICAL POSTURE CONFLICT: Engine B's cardinal invariant is FAIL-OPEN — a missing binary returns abstain('no_tool'), never blocks (engine_b.py:346-359; docs/grounding.md). WS5 requires FAIL-CLOSED for the secrets/High-Critical auto-block. Resolution: the fail-closed decision must live in the CONSUMER (the code-review gate / WS3 decide step) which treats abstain('no_tool'|'timeout'|'other') on a security detector as BLOCK+coverage-gap — NOT inside the oracle. (d) lint.py:33-79 'gitleaks-style' scan is an in-house 8-pattern regex, NOT the gitleaks binary.
